items.title: Xpeng launches new X9 with RM279,888 starting price
items.summary: BERMAX Xpeng Sdn Bhd has introduced the new Xpeng X9, a flagship seven-seat electric multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) designed to combine luxury, advanced technology and intelligent mobility. The new X9 starts from RM279,888 and is positioned as Xpeng's latest offering for families seeking a premium, connected and future-ready driving experience. Bermaz Auto Bhd group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Francis Lee said the launch reflects Xpeng's ambition to make advanced mobility technologies more accessible while strengthening Malaysia's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem.

items.title: Trump: Peace deal with Iran in final stages
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday said negotiations over a peace deal to end the Middle East war were in their final stages, after Iran and Israel halted attacks that threatened to reignite the months-long conflict. Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday in response to strikes against Lebanon's Hizbollah in the southern suburbs of Beirut, before Israel struck back despite Trump's efforts to dissuade Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu from doing so. The Israeli leader said of Iran that the "fire on that front is contained" hours after Teheran said it had stopped its military action, while both sides warned they could resume hostilities despite diplomatic efforts gathering pace.

items.title: Zahid: BN to go solo in Negeri Sembilan after Pakatan lays claim to all 36 seats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election solo, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said BN had no choice but to go solo after Pakatan Harapan (PH) announced its intention to contest all 36 state seats. Ahmad Zahid described the synergy within the existing team as very strong, with all parties working closely together.

items.title: Govt steps up El Nino preparedness as hot, dry weather expected until 2027
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government, through the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), is strengthening the country's level of preparedness following forecasts of more challenging hot and dry weather conditions due to the El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to persist until early 2027. This includes cross-agency cooperation to address any disaster risks arising from the prolonged hot and dry conditions. Nadma director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said that although the situation remains under control, the government is not taking it lightly and has instructed all relevant agencies to enhance their preparedness and implement mitigation measures early to ensure public safety, the continuity of water supply and the nation's well-being.

items.title: Duopharma subsidiary secures RM155.3mil Health Ministry insulin supply contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Duopharma Biotech Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, Duopharma (M) Sdn Bhd (DMSB), has secured a RM155.28 million contract from the Health Ministry to supply recombinant human insulin products and reusable insulin pens to government healthcare facilities. "The contract is in the ordinary course of business of the Duopharma Biotech group of companies and will enable DMSB to supply the products to Health Ministry's facilities. Under the contract, DMSB is required to provide an irrevocable performance bond of RM2.59 million within 30 days of accepting the LOA, Duopharma said.

items.title: Dayang Enterprise forms JV with Brunei partner to expand oil and gas presence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd's (DEHB) wholly-owned subsidiary, Dayang Enterprise Sdn Bhd (DESB), entered into a joint venture (JV) and shareholders agreement with Brunei-based Petrokon Utama Sdn Bhd (PUSB) on May 27 for a proposed project collaboration in Brunei. Under the agreement, DESB and PUSB will incorporate a joint venture company in Brunei, to be known as Petrokon Dayang Corporation Sdn Bhd or such other name as may be approved by the relevant authorities. "DESB shall be entitled to appoint up to two directors to the board of the joint venture company, and one of DESB's nominee directors shall be appointed chairman of the board.

items.title: Bersatu excos can stay in Kedah govt if they back PAS-led administration, says MB
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 9 — Bersatu state executive councillors can remain in the Kedah government despite PAS ending political cooperation with the party. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said the only criterion was that they continue to support the PAS-led state government, news portal Malaysiakini r eported today. Asked whether retaining Bersatu excos contradicted the party's stand, Sanusi said it did not.

items.title: Ringgit closes higher against greenback, regional peers on easing Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against the US dollar and was mostly stronger against regional currencies on Tuesday, supported by improved market sentiment amid easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. At 6pm, the local note appreciated to 4.0580/0630 against the US dollar from Monday's close of 4.0715/0760. SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said that broader regional risk appetite has improved following the tentative truce between Iran and Israel.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 9, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia continued its downward trend on Tuesday, ending the session at 1,675 points. The index was dragged lower by banking and telco stocks, as investors turned cautious ahead of the release of the US May consumer price index data. In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin eased to around RM255,251, while Ethereum also traded lower at the RM6,801 level.

items.title: Parliament must take lead on child rights, says Dewan Rakyat speaker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul said Parliament has a unique role in advancing children's rights, stressing that it requires coordinated action, political will and laws that deliver practical impact. "Parliament's role goes beyond passing laws to include scrutiny, accountability and fostering national dialogue," he said at the Symposium on Children's Rights Legislative Reform in Parliament. One of the strongest messages emerging from the symposium is that protecting children is a shared responsibility, he said.

items.title: Man arrested video clip of hit-and-run involving cyclist goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested the driver of an orange Perodua Myvi involved in a road crash accident that left a young cyclist injured in Kampung Pandan here this morning. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident occurred at 7.26am along Jalan Wirawati 5 and involved a 45-year-old man driving the car and a boy riding a bicycle. Zamzuri said investigators later tracked down the driver.

items.title: New hansard system to improve access to parliamentary records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parliament has introduced a new Hansard management system aimed at improving the efficiency of parliamentary administration through a more integrated approach to recording and accessing official debate records. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the strategic initiative, implemented through GovTech Malaysia, was designed to provide faster and more systematic access to key parliamentary documents while reducing reliance on physical copies. Meanwhile, the Digital Ministry, in a statement, said that the new system transforms the traditional PDF-based Hansard format into a smarter digital platform.

items.title: Malaysia’s fuel subsidy jumped from RM700m in January to RM5b in March before peaking in April, says finance minister II
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Malaysia’s monthly fuel subsidy bill surged to as high as RM7.5 billion in April amid rising global oil prices triggered by tensions in West Asia, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said. He said the subsidy bill rose sharply from about RM700 million a month in January and February to RM5 billion in March before peaking in April, although it has since moderated as oil prices eased. Amir Hamzah said the government’s immediate priority was ensuring uninterrupted fuel supply to support economic stability.

items.title: Wild black bear in Japan captured after multi-day hunt captures the nation's attention
items.summary: UTSUNOMIYA: The Japanese city of Utsunomiya captured a wild black bear on Tuesday after a dramatic multi-day search that gripped the nation, as local schools closed and residents were urged to stay indoors. The city closed all 94 municipal primary and middle schools for a second straight day on Tuesday after its first-ever bear sighting on Saturday evening. Authorities decided to keep schools closed again on Wednesday due to a report of a possible second bear roaming the city, an official said.

items.title: Man arrested for insulting, obstructing traffic cop after summons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a 25-year-old man for allegedly hurling insults at a traffic policeman and obstructing a public servant from carrying out his duties following a traffic enforcement operation in Sentul. Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni said the incident occurred at about 8.24am on June 8 when a policeman conducting traffic enforcement duties issued a summons to the owner of a Proton Prevé for a traffic offence. He said initial investigations found that the suspect admitted recording the policeman because he was unhappy with the enforcement action taken against him.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia ready for next industrial leap with Japan in AI, digital and energy sectors
items.summary: TOKYO, June 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Japanese companies to continue expanding investments in Malaysia, particularly in high-technology sectors such as semiconductors, artificial intelligence, quantum computing and digital industries. Anwar said Malaysia is prioritising emerging technologies such as AI, digital industries and quantum computing while continuing to support traditional sectors including oil and gas. The Prime Minister, who is also Finance Minister, expressed appreciation for the Japanese business community’s long-standing role in Malaysia’s economic development.

items.title: Pas may leave or be expelled from PN after split with Bersatu - Analysts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas may either leave or be expelled from the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, after deciding to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, according to a political analyst. "Pas represents only one voice. He said Pas may form a new political alignment to face the next general election, further fragmenting the opposition bloc.

items.title: Fahmi: 4G coverage to hit 100pc, 5G at 98pc by 2030
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The government is targeting full 4G coverage and 98 per cent 5G coverage in populated areas by 2030, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. Fahmi said current 5G coverage had reached about 84 per cent nationwide, while efforts were ongoing to further expand connectivity across the country. Fahmi said 5G technology was increasingly important for industries as it allowed more devices, equipment and machines to communicate simultaneously compared with 4G and WiFi.

items.title: Cautious sentiment drags Bursa Malaysia lower at the close
items.summary: At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) finished 4.02 points lower at 1,675.50 compared with Monday's close of 1,679.52. Turnover narrowed to 3.76 billion units worth RM2.90 billion from 3.48 billion units worth RM2.92 billion yesterday. ACE Market volume improved to 550.14 million worth RM171.11 million from 545.18 million units valued at RM194.35 million.

items.title: Malaysia keeps fiscal discipline amid subsidy rise, inflation pressures remain contained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is maintaining fiscal discipline and adjusting its spending plans to manage rising global price pressures, even as fuel subsidy costs rose to RM7.5 billion in April. Despite cost pressures stemming from global supply chain disruptions, he said the inflation rate has remained under control, supported by earlier reforms and targeted measures introduced by the government, he added. Amir Hamzah said the government has introduced targeted support measures for affected businesses and individuals.

items.title: Italy probe into Israel's treatment of flotilla activists sparks spat
items.summary: ROME: Italy on Tuesday condemned as "unacceptable" an insult by Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir, following a decision by prosecutors in Rome to investigate him over the treatment of activists in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. Ben Gvir is being investigated for an alleged "war crime" and "torture" after activists accused Israeli authorities of mistreatment during their detention last month, according to Italian media reports. Italian Foreign Minister and Deputy Prime Minister Antonio Tajani told a Senate hearing Tuesday they were "unacceptable words... unworthy of a minister", and which demonstrate "the man's political and moral standing."

items.title: Three arrested over viral video clips of wheelie stunts in Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested three motorcyclists and a pillion rider believed to be involved in separate viral "wheelie" incidents across several roads here following intelligence-led operations. "In the first case, police acted on a 47-second video uploaded on Facebook Reel by "jenterapejuang.my", showing two motorcycles — a Honda EX5 and a Yamaha 135LC FI — performing wheelie stunts along Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and several other locations in Kuala Lumpur. In a separate case, Zamzuri said police investigated another 45-second viral video uploaded on Facebook Reel by "UPDATE&INFO", which showed a Honda EX5 rider performing a wheelie before crashing along Jalan Ampang, Jalan Raja Laut and several other roads in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: No revival of Muafakat Nasional, Zahid says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has ruled out any possibility of reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN), a cooperation once formed between Umno and Pas. Meanwhile, Zahid said Barisan Nasional (BN) would contest all 36 state assembly seats in the Negri Sembilan state election on its own. PH controlled 17 seats through DAP's 11 seats, PKR's five seats and Amanah's one seat.

items.title: Sharper premium electric Zeekr X 2026 bookings open in Malaysia
items.summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Malaysia has opened bookings for the 2026 Zeekr X, an updated premium urban electric SUV. The X features stronger performance, upgraded technology and enhanced comfort features as Zeekr expands its presence in Malaysia's EV market. The refreshed Zeekr X arrives with upgrades across both the Premium and Flagship variants, offering improved performance, enhanced comfort, and new styling elements under the theme "Boldly Yours".

items.title: BN to go solo in Negri Sembilan election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional will contest solo in the Negri Sembilan state election, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. "Yes, in Negri Sembilan BN will definitely go solo because Pakatan Harapan (PH) has announced that it will contest all 36 state assembly seats. Meanwhile, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke recently said the party, together with Pakatan Harapan (PH), would contest all 36 state seats in the state.

items.title: Rival leaders of Turkiye's main opposition party call competing meetings
items.summary: A Turkish court last month annulled the Republican People's Party's (CHP) 2023 congress that elected Ozgur Ozel as chairman, citing irregularities. Both men had previously said they would address the weekly CHP meeting in parliament. Kilicdaroglu then announced a new plan to hold a meeting at party headquarters, also in the capital Ankara.

items.title: Don't blame us to cover up your weaknesses, Bersatu man tells Pas
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A Bersatu member has hit back at Pas Youth chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden, rejecting claims that the party is obstructing unity among the Muslim community. Zulkifli Ibrahim also accused Afnan of misrepresenting decisions made within Perikatan Nasional. He said such remarks risked confusing Pas and Bersatu members as well as PN supporters, who expect leaders to rely on formal decisions rather than political perception.

items.title: 2027 Budget challenge: Managing fiscal deficits while empowering the rakyat
items.summary: LETTERS: Even with greater uncertainty and volatility in the global economic environment, with the disruption in the global supply chain, plus the energy crisis, our economy still managed to grow at a high and commendable rate of 5.4 per cent in the first quarter of this year. Inflation rate is still stable, and the unemployment rate remains low. Investors' confidence to the economy is solid, while the ringgit is performing well vis-a-vis its regional peers.

items.title: ASNB enhances zakat fulfilment via new al-mustaghallat, khultah method
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muslim unitholders of Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (ASNB) fixed-price funds can now opt for the "zakat al-mustaghallat" via "khultah" (collective) payment method. In a statement today, ASNB said that through the khultah method, unitholders can receive net income distributions after a 2.57 per cent zakat deduction, calculated only on the distributed income, not on the investment principal. "Class B is open to Muslim unitholders who wish to manage their zakat more conveniently, where income distributions are received after zakat deduction," it said.

items.title: Loke on alleged PN-Umno letter leak: Let Negri Sembilan voters decide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has brushed aside allegations of a Bersatu-Pas-Umno attempt to topple caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun before the state assembly was dissolved last week. "We have dissolved the state assembly. The Negri Sembilan state assembly was dissolved on June 5 , paving the way for the 16th state election.

items.title: [UPDATED] Fisherman hauls up human skull in net
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: A human skull, believed to belong to a man aged between 20 and 30, was discovered in waters off Sedili after it became entangled in a fishing net on Sunday. District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said police were alerted to the discovery at 8.10pm. The skull was caught in a fishing net by a 44-year-old boat skipper while he was fishing in Sedili waters at 3am the day earlier.

items.title: Govt reviewing 2027 development plans from ministries: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is reviewing development proposals submitted by ministries for 2027 as it moves ahead with the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP). Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry is reviewing the proposed projects and programmes before further assessments begin next month. Akmal said project prioritisation would take into account economic conditions, fiscal considerations and the country's broader development needs.

items.title: AG: No legal breach in Ahmad Terrirudin’s Judicial Appointments Commission role
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Attorney General has maintained that the Malaysian Bar has failed to establish any breach of the Judicial Appointments Commission (JAC) Act in relation to the appointment of Federal Court Judge Tan Sri Ahmad Terrirudin Mohd Salleh as a member of the commission. He said Section 5(1)(e) of the JAC Act sets out only two conditions for JAC membership — that the individual must be a Federal Court judge, and that the appointment must be made by the Prime Minister. He was submitting arguments in opposition to the Malaysian Bar’s application for leave for judicial review challenging Ahmad Terrirudin’s appointment to the JAC.

items.title: Digital Ministry unveils new Hansard system for searchable parliamentary records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 (Bernama) -- Parliament has introduced a new Hansard management system aimed at improving the efficiency of parliamentary administration through a more integrated approach to recording and accessing official debate records. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the strategic initiative, implemented through GovTech Malaysia, was designed to provide faster and more systematic access to key parliamentary documents while reducing reliance on physical copies. Meanwhile, the Digital Ministry, in a statement, said that the new system transforms the traditional PDF-based Hansard format into a smarter digital platform.

items.title: Safe drivers may get up to 65pc insurance savings under Cermat Madani, says transport minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Motorists with good driving records could see total motor insurance savings of up to 65 per cent under the Cermat Madani initiative, which introduces an additional risk-based rebate on top of existing no-claim discounts. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the framework is designed to better reflect driving behaviour in insurance pricing, rewarding low-risk motorists through data-based assessments. “Today, we launch Cermat Madani, an initiative aimed at encouraging a culture of safer, more responsible and disciplined driving through the use of relevant information and data in assessing motor insurance risk,” he said at the launch.

items.title: 'Late Genting heiress cut daughters' RM10 million cash bequests after feeling betrayed'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court was told today by lawyer Datuk Low Beng Choo that the late Genting heiress Lim Siew Kim had felt angry and betrayed by her two daughters and had initially intended to exclude them entirely from her RM1.6 billion estate. Low testified that Siew Kim, the third child of late Genting Group of Companies founder Tan Sri Lim Goh Tong, had originally bequeathed RM10 million in cash to her two daughters in her first will executed on Nov 2, 2021 before later deciding to substantially reduce their inheritances. "She (Siew Kim) said the first plaintiff and her husband were stealing and making false claims from the companies that they were involved in and she felt very betrayed and upset," said Low.

items.title: Immigration Dept detains 30,801 foreigners in nationwide enforcement up to May 31
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — The Immigration Department detained a total of 30,801 foreign nationals for various offences as of May 31 this year. Immigration director-general Zakaria Shaaban said 2,324 of those detained had misused their immigration passes, including by taking part in activities that were not in line with the purpose of their entry into Malaysia. Zakaria said the steps taken include establishing an Enforcement Inspectorate, conducting targeted operations in identified hotspots, taking firm action against employers who violate immigration regulations, and strengthening integrated operations with relevant enforcement agencies.

items.title: Najib posts Onn Hafiz's picture with Chinese community, draws over 2,000 likes
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has weighed in on Johor Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's "No DAP" stance. In a Facebook post, Najib shared a post from BN Johor Facebook page that shows an image of Onn Hafiz interacting with Chinese residents. We are one people, one nation and one shared goal," he posted.

items.title: Human skull believed to be adult male found in fishing net off Sedili waters
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 9 — A human skull believed to belong to an adult male was found in the waters off Sedili near Kota Tinggi after it became entangled in a local fisherman’s net on June 6. Kota Tinggi police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the skull was discovered by a 44-year-old boat skipper at about 3 am before being handed over to the police. He said the skull was subsequently sent to the Forensic Unit of Kota Tinggi Hospital for further examination.

items.title: Hiker Jaslinda looks forward to recovery after nearly two weeks lost in Gunung Batu Putih
items.summary: TAPAH, June 9 — After nearly two weeks lost in the Gunung Batu Putih area and three days in hospital, hiker Jaslinda Saludin says she is looking forward to returning home to rest, while promising to address speculation surrounding her disappearance at a later date. Seen leaving the hospital at about 1.50 pm with a walking stick and her left foot bandaged, Jaslinda said her immediate priority was to recover. Jaslinda also said she would give a statement at an appropriate time to address speculation surrounding her disappearance.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu: Malaysia needs National Food Security Act to tackle supply risks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — The Executive Committee Meeting on the National Food Security Policy No. 1 of 2026, convened today, highlighted the need to introduce the National Food Security Act to strengthen Malaysia’s readiness in addressing food supply issues. The meeting, among others, aimed to chart a more inclusive direction for national food security and recommend a focused strategy to address food security issues, thereby ensuring Malaysia remains resilient amid increasingly dynamic global challenges.

items.title: Perhilitan confirms report of Malayan tiger sighting in Gua Musang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) has confirmed receiving a report regarding the sighting of a Malayan tiger near the Sungai Relai Bridge in Gua Musang, Kelantan, yesterday, following the circulation of a video of the animal on social media. According to a Bernama report, its director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said investigations carried out by the Gua Musang district office found that the tiger was believed to have merely crossed the area before returning to its natural habitat. He added that Perhilitan would continue monitoring the area to ensure public safety and support ongoing conservation efforts for the Malayan tiger.

items.title: Media bodies back national pledge on responsible suicide reporting in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUPUR, June 9 — More than 200 editors, journalists and media professionals have endorsed a national pledge on safe, ethical and responsible reporting of suicide-related content, in a collective industry move aimed at strengthening newsroom practices on sensitive public health coverage. According to organisers, the pledge was signed by representatives from the Malaysian Media Council, the National Union of Journalists and Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia during a programme titled “Reporting Suicide: A Newsroom Guide to Safer Coverage”, jointly organised by the Communications and Multimedia Content Forum of Malaysia (CMCF) and the National Centre of Excellence for Mental Health (NCEMH), Ministry of Health Malaysia, at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre. The signing was witnessed by Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad, underscoring a shared commitment between media practitioners and policymakers to promote safer reporting practices.

items.title: Metal‑cutting caused former Odeon Cinema blaze in Penang that killed Myanmar worker, probe finds
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said forensic investigations found the fire was accidentally triggered during renovation works at the premises. “CCTV footage showed two of the three workers carrying out metal cutting work using an oxy-cutter set before hot molten metal residue fell onto combustible materials below and ignited a fire. Mohamad Shoki said inspections by the department’s K9 Detection Unit found no traces of fire accelerants at the scene.

items.title: Sarawak Water: Tests tonight to trace major leak and determine whether 20,000 or 70,000 Miri consumers face water outage
items.summary: Sarawak Water acting chief executive officer Malseni Jamal said the assessment would involve temporarily shutting off a 750mm valve to establish whether the leak originates from the 750mm pipeline or the larger 1.1 metre pipeline. Malseni said if the leak is traced to the 750mm pipeline, the areas affected would be Taman Jelita, Taman Tunku, Jalan Engkabang, Sungai Rait, Eastwood, Hilltop, and Riam. Other affected areas would include Kampung Beraya, Kuala Sibuti, Bukit Song, Sungai Karab, Bukit Peninjau, Beluru town area, and Sungai Seruas.

items.title: Penang CM says rise in state reserves predates quit rent increase
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today dismissed claims that the state’s cash reserves had risen to more than RM600 million because of the recent quit rent hike, saying the increase was driven by a surplus recorded in 2025. He said the balance in the state’s Consolidated Revenue Account had already increased before the revised quit rent rates came into effect on January 1 this year. Chow said the state recorded a surplus in 2025, with cash reserves exceeding RM500 million as of December 31, 2025.

items.title: Budget 2027 to stay development‑driven amid crisis‑year pressures, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Ministry of Economy will continue to focus on development expenditure in Budget 2027, which is expected to be tabled this October, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said all ministries have submitted their development proposals, and evaluations are being conducted at the ministry level through a bidding mechanism. Akmal Nasrullah said a detailed review of those development plans will begin next month.

items.title: Chow: Penang seeking DoE input before deciding on Jelutong landfill rehabilitation project
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 9 — The Penang government has met the Department of Environment (DoE) to better understand the environmental impact assessment (EIA) considerations for the proposed Jelutong landfill rehabilitation and Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. Chow said the state government’s decision-making process must be guided by verified information and proper procedures. He said the state government must follow the proper process as any decision would have implications, particularly financial ones.

items.title: Mukah police seek public help to find two 14‑year‑old girls missing since Sunday
items.summary: MUKAH, June 9 — Police are seeking help from the public to locate two 14-year-old schoolgirls who were reported missing yesterday. Mukah police chief Muhamad Rizal Alias said Easter Agnes David and Frocilea Rajok Jessica were last seen in Mukah town on Sunday at around 1.30pm. “The two missing girls are identified as Easter Agnes David, an Iban girl from Rumah Jali at Batu 15, Jalan Selangau-Mukah, while Frocilea Rajok Jessica is from Rumah Nelson Chuat Begeri, KM59 Jalan Mukah-Selangau,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Tuaran security guard pleads guilty to sexually assaulting 11‑year‑old girl, sentencing set for June 11
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 9 — The Sessions Court here will sentence a security guard, who pleaded guilty to physically sexually assaulting a minor, on June 11. Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan adjourned the sentencing of Mohd Firdaus Basir, 34, at the request of the prosecution. Mohd Firdaus said every human makes mistakes and appealed for leniency, calling on the court not to impose heavier punishments.

items.title: Pahang Sultan opens first Islamic religious primary school in Cameron Highlands
items.summary: CAMERON HIGHLANDS, June 9 — Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah today officially opened the first Pahang Islamic Religious Primary School (SRAIN) at its temporary premises in Golden Hill, Tanah Rata, Cameron Highlands. His Royal Highness arrived at the school at 10.20 am and was received by Pahang Islamic Religious Department (JAIP) director Datuk Ahmad Khirrizal Ab Rahman. “Our goal is to have a Pahang Islamic Religious Primary School in every district, managed directly by JAIP,” he said.

items.title: Man jailed three months for stealing KTMB track cables in Klang
items.summary: KLANG, June 9 — The Magistrate’s Court here today sentenced a 35-year-old man to three months’ imprisonment after he pleaded guilty to stealing track cables belonging to Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) three days ago. Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat handed down the sentence on Railey Nasal and ordered that it take effect from the date of his arrest. Railey was charged with stealing a 40-metre-long 16-millimetre track cable and a 20-metre-long 2.5-millimetre track cable belonging to KTMB, with an estimated value totalling RM850, from the KTM railway track near Jalan Sembilang 8E, Jalan Telok Pulai, at about 1.20 pm on June 6.

items.title: Malaysia must strengthen fiscal discipline, improve service delivery to attract high‑value investment, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Malaysia must continue to strengthen fiscal discipline, improve public service delivery, support businesses, protect the rakyat and position the country to capture higher-value investment and growth, said Finance Minister II Senator Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said these would ensure that reform is anchored in credibility, accountability and trust. Amir Hamzah said this in his keynote address at the Malaysia Institute of Accountants (MIA) International Accountants Conference 2026 here today.

items.title: Zahid: Kemas children to move seamlessly into national preschools, primary education under new MoU
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 – Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said a new early childhood education pathway linking Kemas childcare and kindergartens to national preschool and primary education is being developed through a strategic collaboration between the Rural and Regional Development Ministry (KKDW) and the Education Ministry (MoE). He said he initiated efforts to position Tabika under Kemas as “feeder kindergartens” for preschools in national schools across the country. “Education for children under the age of five will be under Kemas, while preschool education at age five will be under the Education Ministry.

items.title: Bukit Aman: BTS concert ticket scams leave fans RM95,000 poorer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — The Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) has advised the public to remain vigilant against concert ticket scams involving BTS World Tour that have been detected across various social media platforms. CCID director Comm Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said authorities have detected syndicates and scammers using various online platforms to offer concert tickets, particularly for VIP, Soundcheck and Priority Access categories. “Avoid making payments to individuals offering tickets via social media without verified credentials; and be cautious of claims involving special access, insider allocations or pre-sale tickets before official sales open.

items.title: Over 2.7 million eligible to vote in Johor polls, Election Commission to appoint 42,000 workers
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 9 — The Election Commission (EC) has registered over 2.7 million electors eligible to cast their ballots in the 16th Johor state election, based on the Supplementary Electoral Roll for April 2026, which was gazetted on May 21. He said that approximately 42,000 election workers are expected to be appointed and mobilised to manage all aspects of the state election. The workforce will include 56 Returning Officers, election personnel and security officers involved throughout the election period.

items.title: ‘Just don’t blame Umno’: Khaled on PAS-Bersatu split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin today said the party had no hand in the Islamist party Pas’ decision to end cooperation with Bersatu. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced the decision to terminate political cooperation with Bersatu last night following weeks of internal tussle in the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. Despite terminating political cooperation with Bersatu, PAS is reportedly remaining in the PN camp.

items.title: Muhyiddin: Bersatu staying put in Perikatan despite PAS ending cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu today said it will remain in Perikatan Nasional (PN) despite PAS’ decision to end political cooperation between the two parties. In a statement, Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said the party would continue strengthening the opposition coalition as one of its founding members. He said the decision to end political cooperation and sever ties with Bersatu was made by PAS.

items.title: Johor primary school teacher claims trial to sexual offences involving pupil, possession of obscene material
items.summary: The accused, Mohamad Amirul Awang Meran, who has been teaching for five years, also claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court to two counts of possessing obscene material found on his mobile phones. The accused, who is attached to a primary school in Masai, entered his plea after the charges were read before three judicial officers — Judge Nor Aziati Jaafar, Judge Madihah Zainol and Magistrate Afifah Hazimah Wahab. Judge Nor Aziati set bail at RM10,000, Judge Madihah set bail at RM3,000, and Magistrate Afifah Hazimah set bail at RM6,000.

items.title: Zahid: Umno-PAS talks open constructive space for non-political issues
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 9 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi described the recent discussions between the leadership of Umno and PAS as a positive step towards addressing various issues, including matters not strictly related to politics. He said such engagements should be seen as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen dialogue and explore new approaches amid evolving political dynamics. “We welcome the meeting held between the leadership of Umno and PAS to discuss and resolve several issues and agendas that are not necessarily political in nature.
